explanation: With very few forecasters, the EWMA skill estimates are dominated by estimation noise. The variance of σ̂ is too high relative to the true skill differences, so the skill-weighted aggregate is no better — and sometimes worse — than equal weighting.

The EWMA skill layer requires a burn-in period to converge. With fewer than ~50 rounds, the learned skill weights are still noisy and the mechanism has not had enough observations to separate good from bad forecasters.

When the underlying forecaster quality changes abruptly (regime shift or perturbation), the EWMA skill estimates become stale. During the re-learning window (~50 rounds), the mechanism assigns outdated weights and equal weighting dominates.

When all forecasters have identical intrinsic quality, there is no skill signal to exploit. The skill layer learns near-equal weights with noise, so the mechanism reduces to a noisier version of equal weighting with no accuracy benefit.
